finding: A file sync followed by rename is not the strongest available crash-durability boundary; the containing directory must also be synchronized so the renamed directory entry is requested for persistence.

source_status.linux_fsync: Linux manual page describing fsync durability and directory-entry limits

verification.scope: byte-identical isolated source and deterministic injected directory-sync behavior

uncertainty: No power-loss or kernel crash was induced.
Directory fsync support and semantics vary by operating system and filesystem.
A sync failure occurs after a valid rename, so callers must treat the result as indeterminate rather than retrying a conflicting terminal blindly.
falsification_route: Run crash-injection and power-loss tests on the production filesystem; reject the design if a successful return can lose the renamed journal entry or if a failed return permits a second conflicting terminal.
specialist_registry_change: none; existing Primary Source Scout, Technical Synthesist, Experiment Designer, and Tool Evaluator responsibilities cover this artifact
